title: Stale timestamp calculation loses microsecond precision
description:
File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Line: 349

The stale timestamp is calculated via float conversion:
```python
stale = datetime.fromtimestamp(now.timestamp() + stale_seconds, tz=UTC)
```

datetime.timestamp() returns a float, and floating-point arithmetic can lose microsecond precision. This can cause subtle timing inconsistencies.

Fix: Use timedelta for precise datetime arithmetic:
```python
from datetime import timedelta
stale = now + timedelta(seconds=stale_seconds)
```

Severity: Low - unlikely to cause practical issues with 120-second stale windows, but incorrect as a matter of precision.
